sources.title: Detecting hallucinations in large language models using semantic entropy
sources.citation_caveat: Nature version of record published 2024-06-19. Use the paper PDF as the semantic entropy implementation source; code repositories are auxiliary only.

sources.title: Semantic Energy
sources.citation_caveat: Treat as arXiv preprint / submission-caveated source unless archival publication is explicitly verified.

sources.title: QuCo-RAG
sources.citation_caveat: Use as preprint or status-caveated source unless official archival status is verified.

sources.title: Entropy Alone is Insufficient for Safe Selective Prediction in LLMs
sources.citation_caveat: Use only for reference-only framing around learned combination and probe-assisted selective prediction. Do not implement probe features or hidden-state methods in this repo.

sources.title: Cost-Effective Hallucination Detection for LLMs
sources.citation_caveat: arXiv preprint (2024). Three-step pipeline (score → calibrate → threshold) with multi-scoring framework. Use as positioning reference: their calibration is conditional on input/response *attributes* (internal scoring methods); ours is conditional on *external corpus statistics*.

sources.title: Trust Me, I'm Wrong: LLMs Hallucinate with Certainty Despite Knowing the Answer
sources.citation_caveat: arXiv preprint (Feb 2025, revised Aug 2025). Identifies CHOKE (Certain Hallucinations Overriding Known Evidence) where models hallucinate confidently despite having internal knowledge. Use as motivating evidence for the SE low-diversity wrong answer limitation our framework addresses via corpus axis conditioning.
